articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council asserts that implementing licensing for landlords won't result in increased rent prices for tenants.  
- Landlords face a financial burden of £694 per property, with no plans to recoup these costs.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook officially announces that the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The statement clarifies Labour's position on housing policy amidst ongoing debates about affordability in the UK property market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion

articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to become law in just 10 days, is expected to significantly diminish the presence of hobbyist landlords in the UK rental market.

articles.title: Fewer landlords selling as rental stock gets smaller
articles.summary: The size of the UK rental stock is falling

articles.title: Landlords unhappy with service from letting agents – new data

articles.title: Shock survey says another 5% of private rental stock could go
articles.summary: A specialist mortgage lender has surveyed the private rental sector
articles.ai_summary:

- The findings highlight ongoing challenges in the housing sector as landlords face increasing pressures.

articles.title: Tenant and children put at risk by rogue landlord
articles.summary: He failed to comply with an Improvement Notice served by the council.
articles.ai_summary:

- Despite being legally required to make necessary repairs, the landlord continued to disregard the council's directives, putting vulnerable occupants at risk.

articles.title: Superior landlords can be fined for unlicensed HMOs, legal experts warn

articles.title: BLOG: Will AI eat your job in estate agency? No – and here’s why
articles.summary:

The post BLOG: Will AI eat your job in estate agency? No – and here’s why appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- While AI is set to automate many procedural roles, it won't replace the unique skills and personal touch that professionals in the estate agency field provide.
- The property industry can leverage AI to enhance efficiency and service, ensuring agents remain indispensable in navigating complex transactions.

articles.title: “Bring back Section 21,” insists female director marking 25 years at same agency
